answer:
The problem can be approached using the **Schwarz lemma** and **maximum modulus principle**. Given the condition \(|Rf(z)| \leq |e^z|\) for \(|z| < 1\), the maximum of \(|f(z)|\) on the unit disk is achieved on the boundary \(|z| = 1\). Evaluating \(|e^z|\) at \(z = \frac{1+i}{2}\) (which lies on the boundary of the disk when scaled appropriately) yields \(|e^{(1+i)/2}| = e^{1/2} \approx 1.6487\), but this is too small compared to the options.

Instead, the **maximum modulus principle** suggests that the maximum of \(|f(z)|\) on the closed unit disk is bounded by the supremum of \(|e^z|\) on the boundary \(|z| = 1\). The maximum of \(|e^z|\) on \(|z| = 1\) occurs at
